thesis: Consumer-first telehealth platform with 2.5M+ subscribers across sexual health, mental health, dermatology, and weight loss. Revenue grew 59% YoY to $2.35B in FY2025 with 72% gross margins. Pivoting from compounded GLP-1 drugs to branded medications. 36% short interest creates squeeze potential but also reflects real regulatory risk.

drivers: EBITDA grew 80% in 2025; margin expansion continuing
Platform scalability — fixed costs spread over growing subscriber base
International growth via Eucalyptus acquisition (UK, Germany)
36% short interest could fuel squeeze on positive catalyst

risks: GLP-1 revenue collapse if compounded drugs banned (~25% of revenue at risk)
DOJ investigation could lead to fines or business restrictions
Competition from Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly, Amazon, CVS telehealth
Subscriber churn if GLP-1 customers don't stay for other services
Multiple contraction if revenue growth slows below 20%

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 20–25% annually through 2027; EBITDA margins stabilize 13–15%; international contributes 15–20% by 2028; regulatory issues resolved with manageable impact.

catalysts.event: Q1 2026 Earnings — first quarter post-GLP-1 pivot

key_risks: FDA crackdown on compounded GLP-1 medications
Novo Nordisk patent lawsuit blocking alternative GLP-1 formulations
Margin compression from international expansion and branded drugs
